textDisplay: So yesterday i finally got to watch this movie. While watching it, it gave me the chills and i know it's not just the AC in the theatre (Lol). It also gave me some kind of emotions deep inside me that i just wanted to scream out... And i did...This one scene, it was so majestic and amazing that i clapped and shout and i didn't even hesitated or think before i did it, nor did i ever think if i would look stupid if i was the only one clapping but i was so fucking surprised when i made the whole crowd row by row clap and shout too. Yesterday was literally one i can add as another one of my best  days ever!

"Five years ago I thought I lost you forever. There is no curse that could ever tell me away from you. Will you marry me?"

"Yes"

"Mistress, I have a little bit of news."

"Don't ruin my morning."
"Philip asked me to marry him."

"No."

"I wasn't really asking."

"Nor was I, love doesn't always end well beasty."

"Trust me. Let us prove you wrong."

"You have done an admirable job going against your own nature to raise this child but now she will finally get the love of a real mother. Tonight, I consider Aurora my own."

"There is no union, there will be no wedding!"

"What have you done?"

"We go home now. Aurora!"

"Maleficent is a threat to everyone because you are best to protect you."

"Well well."

"One can never been too careful."

"God Mother! This isn't you!"

"You do not know me."

"Tell me what happened."

"There was another creature."

"You've spent years caring for human. Now it's time to care for your own."

"Dont....ruin my morning!"

I just saw the movie. It was better than I expected, quite a treat 🎃💥🎃. As great as the first one, I'd say. Beautiful and fierce with a strong story line, what can I say. Angie did a great job. Something we did not see on the first movie. You know, technically, Maleficent is the villain. She does violent things out of anger, her first instinct is to fight back, she’s crazy overprotective that it can be unreasonable. And if this were in the point of view of the other kingdom, we’d all see her as the villain. But, hear me out before you start bashing me. She’s the type of villain you can understand. The type of villain who is the way she is for a good reason. We just needed to hear her side of the story, which we did. A lot of us could probably relate to her. the last film stated she’s a villain and a hero. Which makes complete sense. she’s not doing this for dominance or power, or for chaos. she’s doing it because she’s hurt and wants to prove a point. She made a promise to Aurora that she’d protect her for the rest of her life and she’s keeping to that promise. Maleficent is literally the perfect example of a loving mother who wants the best for her child.  Everything she does is just an example of how much she cares.

I’m sorry; this just looks trite. What’s wrong with having a genuinely evil character just be genuinely evil? Why do we need to be sympathetic towards them? The first Maleficent movie ruined the character for me, and just felt like needless pandering for the sake of making a quick buck off Hot Topic customers. They could have made a mature version of Sleeping Beauty, while keeping Maleficent as the female embodiment of pure magical evil, expand upon Aurora’s family and the Prince’s, and give a more compelling story than what we got in the animation. “Maleficent” made the Fairies irrelevant. They dumbed down the gravity of Maleficent‘s curse and her sociopathic pettiness over not being invited. It’s supposed to show how petty and cruel she is, and make her to be this purely evil, yet cunning and methodical villain. Instead we get a dumbed down curse, irrelevant fairies, and even worse; MALEFICENT CARES ABOUT AURORA!! She’s supposed to want her dead, and only the intervention of the last fairy’s blessing lessens the toll from death to sleep. Gods above and below, this is just ridiculous.

Honestly this debate over good or evil is stupid to me. She's flawed. No one is one or the other. If someone does bad there is usually a reason that leads up to it whether we feel it's justified or not. A villain being evil "just because" is boring as hell. Having her trust broken. Feeling betrayed and hurt and lashing out because of it. These actions make more sense to me and makes her character and anger more believable. If you want a 2d character villain with an oversimplified plot, go watch the cartoon to relive your missed childhood and leave this movie to the adults. From the trailer, I'm on Team Maleficent. She may have overreacted but she had justification to be offended by that lady's backhanded comments. ( I will say that I feel she could have cut that bitch up one side and down the other with her words alone rather than getting physical though.) Just because Aurora was too stupid to notice doesn't change the fact that it was an insult. Honestly I feel like she should have been at a children's table or something and left the adults to their business. She is so boring as a character. She supposedly sees Mal as a mother but is okay with the way that woman treated her. If that was me and my mom, I'd be the first one defending her even though I know she wouldn't need me to. Makes me want to smack her.

You can teach them about your own mistakes, but them release them into the world, and if they get back hurt for whatever reason, you welcome them with your own love and help them get up on their feet again. That is true love. What she's doing... Nah, not so much.

@skynikan I know. But what I'm just trying to say is that let us not judge her for what she became, though what she did is not right we still can't fault the pain she'd been through. She just learned reality in a hard way and if becoming jaded was the only salvation she found in order to survive, then do we really have the right to dictate on what's wrong and what isn't? The world isn't white and black, and moral standards are just made to keep our demons on leash, to keep the weak minded oblivious of the real carnage that is happening in the real world. And once you have been touched by darkness you can never undo it anymore, the only way forward is to dance along it while trying to survive.
